children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The inscription of the year of minting or issuance of the coin, the mint mark, and the inscription “E Pluribus Unum” shall be edge-incused into the coin.

children.heading: .—Preservation of distinctive edge
children.label: ii .—Preservation of distinctive edge
children.text: The edge-incusing of the inscriptions under clause (i) on coins issued under this subsection shall be done in a manner that preserves the distinctive edge of the coin so that the denomination of the coin is readily discernible, including by individuals who are blind or visually impaired.

children.heading: Issuance of coins commemorating innovation or innovators.—
children.label: 3 Issuance of coins commemorating innovation or innovators.—

children.heading: .—State
children.label: I .—State
children.text: With respect to each State, the coins shall be issued in the order in which the States ratified the Constitution of the United States or were admitted into the Union, as the case may be.

children.heading: .—District of columbia and territories
children.label: II .—District of columbia and territories
children.text: After all coins are issued under subclause (I), the coins shall be issued for the District of Columbia and the territories in the following order: the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: .—Application in event of the admission of additional states
children.label: ii .—Application in event of the admission of additional states
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (i), if any additional State is admitted into the Union before the end of the 14-year period referred to in paragraph (1), the Secretary of the Treasury may issue a $1 coin with respect to the additional State in accordance with clause (i)(I).

children.heading: .—Application in the event of independence or adding of a territory
children.label: iii .—Application in the event of independence or adding of a territory
I
children.text: if any territory becomes independent or otherwise ceases to be a territory of the United States before $1 coins are minted pursuant to this subsection, the subsection shall cease to apply with respect to such territory; and

children.label: II
children.text: if any new territory is added to the United States, $1 coins shall be issued for such territories in the order in which the new the territories are added, beginning after the $1 coin is issued for the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Issuance of coins commemorating four innovations or innovators during each of 14 years.—
children.label: B Issuance of coins commemorating four innovations or innovators during each of 14 years.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Four $1 coin designs as described in this subsection shall be issued during each year of the period referred to in paragraph (1) until 1 coin featuring 1 innovation, an individual innovator, or a group of innovators, from each of the States, the District of Columbia, and territories has been issued.

children.heading: .—Number of coins of each design
children.label: ii .—Number of coins of each design
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe, on the basis of such factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, the number of $1 coins that shall be issued with each of the designs selected for each year of the period referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Concept
children.label: A .—Concept
children.text: With respect to each State, the District of Columbia, and each territory to be honored with a coin under this subsection, the selection of the significant innovation, innovator, or group of innovators to be borne on the reverse of such coin shall be made by the Secretary of the Treasury, after consultation with the Governor or other chief executive of the State, the District of Columbia, or territory with respect to which a coin is to be issued under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: the Governor or other chief executive of the State, the District of Columbia, or territory with respect to which a coin is to be issued under this subsection; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Commission of Fine Arts; and

children.label: ii
children.text: review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: .—Selection and approval process
children.label: C .—Selection and approval process
children.text: Proposals for designs for $1 coins under this subsection may be submitted in accordance with the design selection and approval process developed by the Secretary in the sole discretion of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Standards
children.label: D .—Standards
children.text: Because it is important that the Nation’s coinage and currency bear dignified designs of which the citizens of the United States can be proud, the Secretary shall not select any frivolous or inappropriate design for any $1 coin minted under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on certain representations
children.label: E .—Prohibition on certain representations
children.text: No head and shoulders portrait or bust of any person and no portrait of a living person may be included in the design of any coin issued under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Treatment as numismatic items
children.label: 5 .—Treatment as numismatic items
children.text: For purposes of sections 5134 and 5136, all $1 coins minted under this subsection shall be considered to be numismatic items.

children.heading: .—Issuance of numismatic coins
children.label: 6 .—Issuance of numismatic coins
children.text: The Secretary may mint and issue such number of $1 coins of each design selected under this subsection in uncirculated and proof qualities as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Termination of program
children.label: 7 .—Termination of program
children.text: The issuance of coins under this subsection shall terminate when one innovation, an individual innovator, or a group of innovators, from each State, the District of Columbia, and each territory has been honored and may not be resumed except by an Act of Congress.

children.heading: 19Redesign and Issuance of Quarter Dollars Emblematic of Prominent American Women and Commemorating theth Amendment.—
children.label: x 19Redesign and Issuance of Quarter Dollars Emblematic of Prominent American Women and Commemorating theth Amendment.—

children.heading: Redesign of quarter dollars beginning in 2022.—
children.label: 1 Redesign of quarter dollars beginning in 2022.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Effective beginning , notwithstanding the fourth sentence of subsection (d)(1) and subsection (d)(2), the Secretary of the Treasury shall issue quarter dollars that have designs on the reverse selected in accordance with this subsection which are emblematic of the accomplishment of a prominent American woman.January 1, 2022

children.heading: .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
children.label: B .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
i
children.text: the inscription described in the second sentence of subsection (d)(1) appears on the reverse side of any such quarter dollar; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any of the inscriptions described in the third sentence of subsection (d)(1) or the designation of the value of the coin appear on the obverse side of any such quarter dollar.

children.heading: .—Single prominent american woman on each quarter dollar
children.label: C .—Single prominent american woman on each quarter dollar
children.text: The design on the reverse side of each quarter dollar issued under this subsection shall be emblematic of the accomplishments and contributions of one prominent woman of the United States, and may include contributions to the United States in a wide spectrum of accomplishments and fields, including but not limited to suffrage, civil rights, abolition, government, humanities, science, space, and arts, and should honor women from ethnically, racially, and geographically diverse backgrounds.

children.heading: .—Issuance of quarter dollars emblematic of up to five prominent american women each year
children.label: D .—Issuance of quarter dollars emblematic of up to five prominent american women each year
children.text: The designs for the quarter dollars issued during each year of the period of issuance described under paragraph (4) shall be emblematic of up to five prominent American women.

children.heading: .—Selection of prominent american women generally
children.label: E .—Selection of prominent american women generally
i
children.text: in accordance with a selection process developed by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: after soliciting recommendations from the general public for prominent women designs for quarter dollars; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in consultation with the Smithsonian Institution American Women’s History Initiative, National Women’s History Museum, and the Bipartisan Women’s Caucus.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: All designs under this subsection shall be selected by the Secretary, after consultation with the Commission of Fine Arts and review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: .—Obverse
children.label: B .—Obverse
children.text: The design on the obverse of the quarter dollars shall maintain a likeness of George Washington, and be designed in a manner, such as with incused inscriptions, so as to distinguish it from the obverse design used during the previous quarters program.

children.heading: .—Issuance of coins
children.label: 3 .—Issuance of coins
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe, on the basis of such factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, the number of new designs during each year of the period of issuance, and the number of coins which shall be issued with each of the designs selected for such year.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The program established under this subsection shall continue in effect until the end of 2025.

children.heading: .—Continuity
children.label: B .—Continuity
children.text: After 2025, the Secretary may continue to issue coins minted during the program but not yet issued.

children.heading: Redesign and Issuance of Coins Emblematic of the United States Semiquincentennial.—
children.label: y Redesign and Issuance of Coins Emblematic of the United States Semiquincentennial.—
i
children.text: Notwithstanding the 4th, 5th, and 6th sentences of subsection (d)(1), the Secretary may change the design on any of the coins authorized under this section and minted for issuance during the one-year period beginning , in celebration of the United States semiquincentennial.January 1, 2026

children.label: ii
children.text: Notwithstanding the 2nd and 3rd sentences of subsection (d)(1), the Secretary may place the required inscriptions on either the obverse or reverse sides of the coins authorized for redesign under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Quarter dollars
children.label: B .—Quarter dollars
children.text: The Secretary may issue quarter dollars in 2026 with up to five different designs emblematic of the United States semiquincentennial. One of the quarter dollar designs must be emblematic of a woman’s or women’s contribution to the birth of the Nation or the Declaration of Independence or any other monumental moments in American History.

children.heading: .—Dollars
children.label: C .—Dollars
children.text: The Secretary may, in addition to the coins produced under subsections (r) and (w), mint for issuance during the one-year period beginning , $1 dollar [sic] coins with designs emblematic of the United States semiquincentennial.January 1, 2026

children.heading: .—Designs after end of the program
children.label: D .—Designs after end of the program
children.text: Beginning in 2027, any coin redesigned under this subsection shall revert to the immediately previous designs, with the exception of the quarter dollar and the half dollar, which shall bear designs in accordance with subsection (z).

children.heading: .—Redesign definition
children.label: E .—Redesign definition
children.text: A redesign authorized under this subsection shall not constitute a “change” for purposes of subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Each of the designs authorized under this subsection shall be selected by the Secretary after consultation with Commission of Fine Arts and review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: .—Design selection process
children.label: B .—Design selection process
children.text: Designs shall be developed and selected in accordance with the design selection process developed by the Secretary in consultation with the United States Semiquincentennial Commission and with recommendations from the general public.

children.heading: Redesign and Issuance of Quarter Dollars and Half Dollars Emblematic of Sports Played by American Youth.—
children.label: z Redesign and Issuance of Quarter Dollars and Half Dollars Emblematic of Sports Played by American Youth.—

children.heading: Redesign of quarter dollars beginning in 2027.—
children.label: 1 Redesign of quarter dollars beginning in 2027.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Effective beginning , notwithstanding the fourth sentence of subsection (d)(1) and subsection (d)(2), the Secretary shall issue quarter dollars that have designs on the reverse selected in accordance with this subsection which are emblematic of sports played by American youth.January 1, 2027

children.heading: .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
children.label: B .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
i
children.text: the inscription described in the second sentence of subsection (d)(1) appears on the reverse side of any such quarter dollars; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any of the inscriptions described in the third sentence of subsection (d)(1) or the designation of the value of the coin appear on the obverse side of any such quarter dollars.

children.heading: .—Single sport on each quarter dollar
children.label: C .—Single sport on each quarter dollar
children.text: The design on the reverse side of each quarter dollar issued under this subsection shall be emblematic of one sport played by American youth.

children.heading: .—Issuance of quarter dollars emblematic of up to five sports each year
children.label: D .—Issuance of quarter dollars emblematic of up to five sports each year
children.text: The designs for the quarter dollars issued during each year of the period referred to in paragraph (5) shall be emblematic of up to five sports.

children.heading: .—Selection of sports generally
children.label: E .—Selection of sports generally
children.text: The Secretary shall select the sports to be honored during each year of the period referred to in paragraph (5) after appropriate outreach and consultation with the public.

children.heading: Redesign of half dollars beginning in 2027.—
children.label: 2 Redesign of half dollars beginning in 2027.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Effective , notwithstanding the fourth sentence of subsection (d)(1) and subsection (d)(2), the Secretary shall issue half dollars that have designs on the reverse selected in accordance with this subsection which are emblematic of a sport tailored to athletes with a range of disabilities, including physical impairment, vision impairment and intellectual impairment (referred to in this Act as a “Paralympic” sport).January 1, 20274So in original. Probably should be “this subsection”.4

children.heading: .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
children.label: B .—Flexibility with regard to placement of inscriptions
i
children.text: the inscription described in the second sentence of subsection (d)(1) appears on the reverse side of any such half dollars; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any of the inscriptions described in the third sentence of subsection (d)(1) or the designation of the value of the coin appear on the obverse side of any such half dollars.

children.heading: .—Single paralympic sport on each half dollar
children.label: C .—Single paralympic sport on each half dollar
children.text: The design on the reverse side of each half dollar issued under this subsection shall be emblematic of one Paralympic sport.

children.heading: .—Selection of sports
children.label: D .—Selection of sports
children.text: The selection of a Paralympic sport to be honored with a half dollar under this subsection shall be made by the Secretary after consultation with U.S. Paralympics.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: All designs under this subsection shall be selected by the Secretary, after consultation with the Commission of Fine Arts and review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: .—Quarter dollar obverse
children.label: B .—Quarter dollar obverse
children.text: The design on the obverse of the quarter dollars shall maintain a likeness of George Washington, and be designed in a manner so as to distinguish it from the obverse design used during the previous quarter dollars program.

children.heading: .—Half dollar obverse
children.label: C .—Half dollar obverse
children.text: The design on the obverse of the half dollar shall maintain a likeness of John Kennedy, and be designed in a manner so as to distinguish it from the obverse design used on the current half dollar.

children.heading: .—Quarter dollar
children.label: A .—Quarter dollar
children.text: The quarter dollar coins bearing designs under this subsection shall be issued at the rate of up to 5 new designs during each year of the period of issuance described under paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Half dollar
children.label: B .—Half dollar
children.text: The half dollar coins bearing designs under this subsection shall be issued at the rate of 1 new design during each year of the period of issuance described under paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The program established under this subsection shall continue in effect until the end of 2030.

children.heading: .—Continuity
children.label: B .—Continuity
children.text: After the date specified in subparagraph (A), the Secretary may continue to issue coins minted during the program but not yet issued.

children.heading: .—Accompanying sports medals
children.label: 6 .—Accompanying sports medals
children.text: For every design of a coin honoring a sport issued under this subsection, the Secretary is authorized to design and issue one or more accompanying medals with designs emblematic of the sport honored with the issuance of the coin, and include a surcharge on the sale the medals sold in accordance with this paragraph, in an amount determined by the Secretary, in the Secretary’s sole discretion, that may be used for the design and manufacture of the medals described in paragraph (7).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to design and manufacture medals for award at the 2028 Olympic Games in Los Angeles, California.

children.heading: .—Working stock
children.label: B .—Working stock
children.text: The Secretary may use Treasury working gold and silver stock in the manufacture of the award medals produced under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Olympic & paralympic committees
children.label: C .—Olympic & paralympic committees
children.text: The Secretary may provide the medals described in this paragraph to the United States Olympic & Paralympic Committee under terms and conditions established by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Cooperative marketing and promotion opportunities
children.label: D .—Cooperative marketing and promotion opportunities
children.text: The Secretary is encouraged to seek out cooperative marketing and promotion opportunities, including with the United States Olympic & Paralympic Committee, LA28, and United States Olympic and Paralympic Properties to promote the coins and medals produced under this section.

children.heading: .—Obverse
children.label: i .—Obverse
children.text: The obverse of the quarter dollar shall bear a design containing a likeness of George Washington.

children.heading: .—Reverse
children.label: ii .—Reverse
children.text: The reverse of the quarter dollar shall be of a design selected by the Secretary after consultation with the Commission of Fine Arts and review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: .—Obverse
children.label: i .—Obverse
children.text: The obverse of the half dollar shall bear a design containing a likeness of John Kennedy.

children.heading: .—Reverse
children.label: ii .—Reverse
children.text: The reverse of the half dollar shall be of a design selected by the Secretary after consultation with the Commission of Fine Arts and review by the Citizens Coinage Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Standards and General Provisions for Circulating Collectible Coins Under Subsections (x), (y), and (z).—
children.label: aa Standards and General Provisions for Circulating Collectible Coins Under Subsections (x), (y), and (z).—

children.heading: .—Prohibition on certain representations
children.label: 1 .—Prohibition on certain representations
children.text: No head and shoulders portrait or bust of any person, living or dead, and no portrait of a living person may be included in the design on the reverse of any coin under subsections (x), (y), and (z).

children.heading: .—Treatment as numismatic items
children.label: 2 .—Treatment as numismatic items
children.text: For purposes of sections 5134 and 5136, all coins and medals minted under subsections (x), (y), and (z) shall be considered to be numismatic items.

children.heading: .—Quality of coins
children.label: A .—Quality of coins
children.text: The Secretary may mint and issue such number of coins of each design selected under subsections (x), (y), and (z) in uncirculated and proof qualities as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Coordination
children.label: B .—Coordination
children.text: The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and the Secretary shall take steps to ensure that an adequate supply of coins produced under subsections (x), (y), and (z) are available for commerce and collectors at such places and in such quantities as are appropriate.

children.heading: .—Number of each coin designs in each year
children.label: C .—Number of each coin designs in each year
children.text: Of the coins issued during each year of the period of issuance under subsections (x), (y), and (z), the Secretary shall prescribe, on the basis of such factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, the number of coins which shall be issued with each of the designs selected for such year.

children.heading: .—Special inscriptions or symbol across the coins
children.label: D .—Special inscriptions or symbol across the coins
children.text: The Secretary is encouraged to develop and include on any coin issued in accordance with subsections (x), (y), or (z), a unifying inscription, privy mark, or other symbol for that particular coin program.

children.heading: .—Legal tender
children.label: 4 .—Legal tender
children.text: The coins minted under subsections (x), (y), and (z) shall be legal tender, as provided in section 5103.

children.heading: .—Marketing and educational campaign
children.label: 5 .—Marketing and educational campaign
children.text: In an effort to advance the collecting of the coins and medals authorized under subsections (x), (y), and (z), and numismatics in general, the Secretary may develop and execute a marketing, advertising, promotional, and educational program to promote the collecting of the coins and medals authorized under subsections (x), (y), and (z). As part of this program, the Secretary is encouraged to seek out appropriate cooperative marketing opportunities, and to develop ancillary derivative products beyond traditional numismatic products such as sports, women, and youth oriented products appropriate to the particular coin and medal program.

children.heading: .—Quality of medals
children.label: 6 .—Quality of medals
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the medals authorized under subsection (z) be produced in high relief and, if feasible and cost effective, with surface treatments such as frosting and colorization.
